title: context always-on rules AGENTS.md
purpose: E4.1 defines context always on rules agents md for the agent-driven expert system. Knowledge & composition — catalog, compose-first, staleness.
scope: Owns `E4.1` only; siblings under `E4` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §7 — Branch E — Knowledge & composition plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#7-branch-e-knowledge-composition-plane).
behavior_steps: At company instantiation, program-scoper binds `state.company.pack_id` and resolves roles, pipelines, governance, and verify suites only from declared paths under `template-packs/`—consumer repos instantiate packs rather than silently forking company.yaml or role YAML into undocumented repo locations.

On every wake, compose-first routing ([B4.3](B4.3-compose-first-catalog-before-improvise.md)) and import resolution ([F5.1](F5.1-cross-pack-imports-micro-packs.md)) reject organizational authority referenced outside the template-packs namespace before the conductor assigns workers or advances `next_action`.

When operators need an urgent organizational change, pursuit records a pack version bump, cross-pack import, or explicit policy override in the journal—not a permanent shadow copy that bypasses catalog and SEC-14 auditability.

Mature patterns promoted from product pursuit must land as pack fragments via Plane D export ([D4.6](D4.6-platform-work-pack-fragment-export.md)); consumer-only role or pipeline edits cannot satisfy maturity claims or goal_verify suites tied to the pack ceiling.

If S0 or validate-workflow detects pack-defined authority living outside `template-packs/`, pursuit stops at H2 with the offending path recorded in journal and state.json until operators restore ceiling compliance or approve a pack update at H1.

Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Map `E4.1` to v2.14â€“v2.23 release row in SEC-15-index.md.

Add unit test under tests/unit/test_e4_1.py when script exists.

Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `E4.1`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: E4.1 defines context always on rules agents md for the agent-driven expert system. Knowledge & composition — catalog, compose-first, staleness. In practice, the system at company instantiation, program-scoper binds `state.company.pack_id` and resolves roles, pipelines, governance, and verify suites only from declared paths under `template-packs/`—consumer repos instantiate packs rather than silently forking company.yaml or role yaml into undocumented repo locations, then on every wake, compose-first routing ([b4.3](b4.3-compose-first-catalog-before-improvise.md)) and import resolution ([f5.1](f5.1-cross-pack-imports-micro-packs.md)) reject organizational authority referenced outside the template-packs namespace before the conductor assigns workers or advances `next_action`.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-e 2026-06-28 -->

Always-on context layers inject project rules and `AGENTS.md` every session—the baseline conductor contract, evidence rules, approval gates, and pipeline semantics. Workers inherit applicable rules via spawn context; they do not re-read the full AGENTS monolith unless allowed.

This is layer zero of context retrieval—below hooks ([E4.2](E4.2-context-hooks-inject-continue-start.md)) and Librarian allowed_reads ([E4.3](E4.3-context-allowed-reads-cap-max-5.md)). Pack authors may extend always-on rules through template-pack fragments. Changes to AGENTS.md mark dependent playbooks and skills stale in [E5.2](E5.2-staleness-extend-playbooks-scripts-pack-nodes.md).
